goal: orch-btn-clicks: Fix the issue that many buttons don't respond to the user's click across the React app (board chrome, project cards/lists, drag-and-drop surfaces). Every spawned role uses model composer-2-fast. Before editing product code, read `.orchestrate/orch-btn-clicks-7894/discovery/BUTTON-CLICK-AUDIT.md` from the bootstrap worker. Do not revert sibling agents' work — stay inside assigned pathsAllowed.
summary: Find and fix click-eating overlays, DnD-guard blocks on real buttons, and card-as-link hit-target stacking so primary controls reliably receive pointer events.
acceptanceCriteria: Root planner discovery is reflected in `discovery/BUTTON-CLICK-AUDIT.md` (bootstrap) with prioritized causes and file-level pointers.
Implementation workers land fixes with regression tests where feasible; Jest + tsc stay green on touched slices.
Each verifier independently confirms its target slice against the audit recommendations and records a strong verification class or a concrete blocker.

tasks.name: bootstrap-btn-clicks-ref
tasks.scopedGoal: Produce the single descendant-facing reference: `.orchestrate/orch-btn-clicks-7894/discovery/BUTTON-CLICK-AUDIT.md` cataloging every likely click-swallowing pattern in the repo (stacking/`::before`/`::after` card-as-link overlays, z-index, `pointer-events`, `@hello-pangea/dnd` interactive blocking per AGENTS.md, AntD Drawer/Modal mask quirks, invisible full-bleed wrappers) with repo-relative paths and line anchors where possible, plus a prioritized fix order for sibling workers.
tasks.brief:
## Must read
- `AGENTS.md` (DnD + TaskModal gotchas).
- `src/pages/board.tsx` (`ColumnsViewport` already documents intentional `pointer-events: none` on fades — cite as good pattern).
- `src/components/projectCard/index.tsx` (card-as-link `TitleLink::after` covering the card; `MetaRow` uses `z-index: 1` — audit whether every interactive child is above the pseudo-layer or whether some controls are still dead).
- Grep `src/` for `Draggable`, `Drag`, `disableInteractiveElementBlocking`, `onClick`, `::after`, `::before`, `pointer-events`, `z-index`, `inset: 0`.

## Deliverable
Create/update **only** files under `.orchestrate/orch-btn-clicks-7894/discovery/**`. The markdown must include:
1. **Executive summary** (5 bullets max) of the most probable regressions.
2. **Inventory table**: Surface | Symptom | Suspected root | Files | Suggested fix pattern.
3. **DnD audit**: list every `Draggable`/`Drag` whose child tree includes native interactive elements (`button`, `input`, etc.) and whether `disableInteractiveElementBlocking` is already set.
4. **Stacking audit**: list pseudo-elements and positioned wrappers that span interactive areas without `pointer-events: none`.
5. **Handoff**: standard orchestrate handoff sections (`## Branch`, `## What I did`, `## Measurements` if any).

Do not change application `src/**` in this task — reference only.
acceptance: `BUTTON-CLICK-AUDIT.md` exists and is substantive (inventory + DnD + stacking sections).
Handoff names the audit path and highlights top 3 fixes for implementers.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- None.

## Automated
- `test -f .orchestrate/orch-btn-clicks-7894/discovery/BUTTON-CLICK-AUDIT.md`
- `git diff --stat` shows only paths under `.orchestrate/orch-btn-clicks-7894/discovery/`

## Manual
- Skim the audit for actionable line pointers.

## Gotchas
- This worker is read-only for product code; do not "helpfully" patch `src`.

tasks.name: fix-hit-target-stacking
tasks.scopedGoal: Eliminate dead clicks on buttons and menus where card-as-link or stretched pseudo-elements steal pointer events; normalize stacking (`z-index`, `position`, `pointer-events: none` on decorative layers only) on project surfaces called out in the bootstrap audit (minimum: `projectCard`, `projectList`, `projectPopover` if in scope).
tasks.brief:
Read `handoffs/bootstrap-btn-clicks-ref.md` (or the audit file path from that handoff) and `.orchestrate/orch-btn-clicks-7894/discovery/BUTTON-CLICK-AUDIT.md`.

## Implementation guidance
- Prefer **not** removing the card-as-link pattern: keep accessibility, but ensure every real control sits above the stretched hit target OR mark the decorative stretch with `pointer-events: none` where semantically correct.
- Any `::after` / `::before` used only for visuals should not intercept clicks intended for buttons.
- Add or extend RTL/Jest tests that click auxiliary controls (kebab, like, etc.) and assert handlers fire (use `userEvent` / `fireEvent` per existing suite style).

## Scope
Touch only files you need under your `pathsAllowed`. If the audit points to additional files in this slice, include them in a follow-up handoff note rather than breaching `pathsForbidden`.

## i18n
Only if new visible strings (unlikely); otherwise skip.
acceptance: Buttons/menus on project cards/lists respond to click in dev and in new/updated tests.
No regression to keyboard focus order or link navigation for the primary card target.
CI test slice for touched components passes.
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- `npm install` if needed.

## Automated
- `CI=true npm test -- --watchAll=false --runInBand src/components/projectCard src/components/projectList src/components/projectPopover`
- `npm run eslint -- --max-warnings 0` on touched files
- `npx tsc --noEmit`

## Manual
- Optional: `npm run dev`, click like/menu/edit on a project card.

## Gotchas
- `MetaRow` already uses `z-index: 1`; verify children aren't creating new stacking contexts that drop below the link overlay.

tasks.name: verify-fix-hit-target-stacking
tasks.scopedGoal: Independently verify hit-target / stacking fixes for project surfaces against the bootstrap audit and worker acceptance criteria.
tasks.brief: Re-run the worker verify recipe on the worker branch. Re-read the relevant audit rows. Confirm tests cover the previously dead controls. Emit `unit-test-verified` on a clean Jest run or explain the blocker.
acceptance: Verifier confirms auxiliary controls are clickable per tests or documented manual evidence.
Verifier records a strong verification class or a precise blocker.

tasks.name: fix-dnd-interactive-clicks
tasks.scopedGoal: Fix board Kanban interactions where `@hello-pangea/dnd` suppresses clicks on native interactive elements inside draggables; align with AGENTS.md (`disableInteractiveElementBlocking` where appropriate) for task cards, column handles, and related drag wrappers.
tasks.brief:
Read the bootstrap audit DnD section and `AGENTS.md` drag-and-drop notes.

## Tasks
1. Inventory `src/components/column/**`, `src/components/dragAndDrop/**`, and any task-card presentation under `src/components/column/` or shared drag helpers.
2. For each `Draggable`/`Drag` root that wraps or fronts a `button`, `input`, `textarea`, `select`, etc., either:
   - move the interactive element outside the blocked subtree, or
   - pass `disableInteractiveElementBlocking` on the library component (document why in a short code comment only if non-obvious).
3. Add regression tests (mount column/task card, `userEvent.click` on a control that was previously dead) following existing `@hello-pangea/dnd` test patterns in the repo.

## Out of scope
- Do not change reorder mutation semantics or `useDragEnd` logic.
acceptance: Identified drags no longer block clicks on legitimate embedded controls called out in audit.
Tests prove at least one previously failing control class now receives clicks.

tasks.name: fix-shell-overlay-clicks
tasks.scopedGoal: Fix remaining chrome-level click swallowing on the board shell, header, copilot/column viewport wrappers, and shared layout surfaces identified in the audit (excluding slices owned by sibling workers).
tasks.brief:
Read the bootstrap audit stacking/overlay rows.

## Likely targets (confirm against audit before editing)
- `src/pages/board.tsx` (shell layers around `ColumnsViewport`, swipe hint, AI chrome — only if audit shows a blocker; preserve intentional `pointer-events: none` on gradient fades).
- `src/layouts/mainLayout.tsx`, `src/components/header/**`, `src/components/copilotShell/**`, `src/components/copilotWelcomeBanner/**`, `src/components/commandPalette/**` if they install invisible interceptors.

## Requirements
- Each change should tie to a cited audit row.
- Add tests where RTL can assert click reaches handlers (e.g., header buttons, command palette) — follow existing strict test patterns.
- Do not modify `src/components/column/**` (sibling worker) unless audit proves shared file conflict; if conflict, document in handoff for planner merge.
acceptance: Audit-cited shell/header/copilot/command issues resolved without breaking DnD or layout min-width fixes in `MainLayout`.
Tests or documented manual evidence for at least one fixed control path.
Lint + tsc clean for touched files.
